factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable as of the latest reporting periods.

evidence.text: Net debt to total capital was 42% at March 31, 2019 and 43% at December 31, 2018.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving as evidenced by cash flows from operations and existing cash resources.

evidence.text: In addition to cash flows from operations, we also had $205.3 million of cash and cash equivalents at March 31, 2019.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its capital expenditure outlook, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We have contractual obligations for long-term debt, operating leases, purchase obligations, and certain other long-term liabilities that are summarized in the table of Contractual Obligations in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2018.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining shareholder returns through consistent dividend payments and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We returned capital to our shareholders by paying $45.8 million of dividends on our common stock and using $10.0 million of cash for share repurchases in the first three months of 2019.

factors.summary: Interest rates are impacting the company's borrowing costs, with terms tied to LIBOR and applicable margins.

evidence.text: The interest rate applicable to borrowings under the Term Loan Agreement is generally either adjusted LIBOR plus an applicable margin (determined by a ratings based grid) or the alternate base rate.

factors.summary: Overall demand appears to be stable with positive organic growth.

evidence.text: Net sales of $1,087.3 million in the first quarter of 2019 increased ten percent compared to the first quarter of 2018 due to the contribution of net sales from acquisitions and higher organic volume.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is present and contributing positively to net sales.

evidence.text: Acquisitions added five percentage points to net sales from the acquisition of Aclara, and organic net sales growth contributed five percentage points, including favorable price realization.

factors.summary: The company is facing rising material costs but is managing them through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Because material costs are approximately two-thirds of our cost of goods sold, volatility in this area can significantly impact profitability. Our goal is to have pricing and productivity programs that offset material and other inflationary cost increases as well as pay for investments in key growth areas.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Executive Overview of the Business

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are ongoing and aim to optimize the manufacturing footprint and cost structure.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and related efforts include the consolidation of manufacturing and distribution facilities, and workforce actions, as well as streamlining and consolidating our back-office functions.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Executive Overview of the Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are ongoing to optimize costs.

factors.summary: Productivity improvement initiatives are a key focus, contributing to cost efficiency.

evidence.text: Productivity improvement also continues to be a key area of focus for the Company and efforts to drive productivity complement our restructuring and related activities to minimize the impact of rising material costs and administrative cost inflation.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Executive Overview of the Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvement initiatives contribute to cost efficiency.

factors.summary: Gross margins are under slight pressure but adjusted margins show improvement.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin declined by 20 basis points to 28.3% in the first quarter of 2019 , driven by cost of goods sold discussed above. Excluding amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets, the adjusted gross profit margin was 28.8% in the first quarter of 2019 as compared to 29.6% in the first quarter of 2018 .

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are under slight pressure but adjusted margins show improvement.

factors.summary: Construction and infrastructure spending is driving demand, particularly in the Electrical segment.

evidence.text: Net sales of our Commercial and Industrial and Construction and Energy business groups increased by two percentage points, due to approximately three percentage points of organic net sales growth, partially offset by a one percentage point headwind from foreign exchange. Organic net sales growth of these businesses was driven primarily by our products serving the industrial, natural gas distribution, and non-residential markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction and infrastructure spending is driving demand.

factors.summary: Aftermarket service revenue is growing, particularly in the Lighting business group.

evidence.text: Net sales of our Lighting business group increased approximately two percent in the first quarter of 2019 driven by favorable price realization. Within the Lighting business group, net sales of residential lighting and commercial and industrial lighting products each increased by two percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Aftermarket service revenue is growing, particularly in the Lighting business group.

factors.summary: Market share is likely stable or improving due to strategic acquisitions and expansion into new markets.

evidence.text: The acquisition extended the Power segment's capabilities into smart automation technologies, accelerates ongoing innovation efforts to address utility customer demand for data and integrated solutions, and expands the segment's reach to a broader set of utility customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Market share is likely stable or improving due to strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: The overall economic environment is favorable, with construction activity and infrastructure spending supporting demand.

evidence.text: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's products, particularly in non-residential and industrial applications.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Executive Overview of the Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable economic conditions with construction activity supporting demand.

factors.summary: The Aclara acquisition has been successful in expanding capabilities and market reach, contributing positively to both net sales and operating income in the Power segment.

evidence.text: The acquisition extended the Power segment's capabilities into smart automation technologies, accelerates ongoing innovation efforts to address utility customer demand for data and integrated solutions, and expands the segment's reach to a broader set of utility customers.

Net sales in the Power segment in the first quarter of 2019 were $457.1 million , up approximately 23% as compared to the first quarter of 2018 primarily due to acquisitions, which contributed approximately 14 percentage points to net sales growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful acquisition has expanded capabilities and market reach, contributing positively to net sales and operating income.
